policies.broad_gap: The backend does not invent a wall-clock or unmanaged receive-order throttling policy, so broader vendor-style update-rate semantics remain out of scope.

broad_spec_rows.notes: The backend now proves explicit and FOM-declared update-rate defaults across direct, inherited, and regioned subscriptions, plus receive-order non-suppression when no logical-time basis exists. Broader vendor-style update-rate policies remain outside the current model.
broad_spec_rows.title: RTI shall support subscribing with update rate reduction where applicable.

broad_spec_rows.notes: The backend proves explicit and FOM-declared update-rate throttling for direct, inherited, and regioned subscriptions, plus receive-order non-suppression when no logical-time basis exists. Broader vendor-style update-rate policies remain outside the current model, so this row remains partial.
broad_spec_rows.title: RTI shall honor update-rate reduction when reflecting attribute updates.
broad_spec_rows.vendor_notes: shared update-rate scenario verifies Pitch reduces reflected update cadence to the negotiated rate

policies.supported_behavior: Update-rate reduction is implemented as logical-time-based throttling. Explicit and FOM-declared update-rate designators apply across direct, inherited, and regioned subscriptions when there is a logical-time basis.
policies.title: Logical-time update-rate subset

policies.broad_gap: The backend does not model arbitrary custom transportation-type behavior beyond the reliable/best-effort subset.

broad_spec_rows.notes: This broad standards row remains partial. The backend now proves distinct reliable versus best-effort callback transport behavior for both FOM-defined defaults and explicit service-selected overrides, plus restore persistence for the supported subset, but it still does not model the full custom transportation semantic space described by the standard.

policies.broad_gap: Message combination, packaging, and passelization are not explicitly modeled, so the permissive broad row stays partial by policy.

broad_spec_rows.notes: This backend proves externally visible delivery semantics for unbatched callbacks. It does not explicitly model message combination, packaging, or passelization, so this row remains partial by policy until those semantics are modeled or separately justified.
broad_spec_rows.title: RTI may combine, package, or passelize messages without changing externally visible semantics.
